title: Delicate Korean Pomegranate Jjigae
description: A velvety, light Gyeongsang-inspired stew featuring pomegranate molasses and delicate spheres, served as a fresh banchan in a paella pan.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in a paella pan over medium heat.
Add tofu cubes and sauté until golden and lightly browned.
Stir in grated ginger and minced garlic, cooking until fragrant.
Pour in 1 cup vegetable broth, 3 tbsp pomegranate molasses, 1 tsp soy sauce, and 1 tsp gochugaru, stirring well.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and cook for 10 minutes to blend flavors.
Meanwhile, dissolve 0.2g sodium alginate in 200ml water, let rest 30 minutes.
Mix 50ml pomegranate juice with 0.1g calcium lactate dissolved in 100ml water to create a calcium bath.
Using a syringe, drop small spoonfuls of the alginate solution into the calcium bath, forming translucent pomegranate spheres that set in 1–2 minutes.
Remove the spheres with a slotted spoon, rinse briefly in cold water, and set aside on a paper towel.
Gently place the spheres on top of the simmering stew and let them warm for 1 minute.
Finish by drizzling a small amount of sesame oil over the stew and garnishing with a sprinkle of chopped scallions.
Serve hot, enjoying the delicate burst of pomegranate spheres in each spoonful.
